title: Supreme Court Seems Open to Trump Request to Block Asylum Seekers at Border
summary: A policy of turning back many asylum seekers at the border was rescinded in 2021, but the Justice Department wants the flexibility to reinstate it as a tool for border control.

title: Gregory Bovino’s Final Days: Harsh Words and Few Regrets
summary: He was the face of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown. But as he begins a retirement that was not entirely voluntary, the Border Patrol leader says he did not go far enough.

title: Minnesota Prosecutors Sue to Access Evidence in Shootings by Agents During Immigration Crackdown
summary: The federal government has refused to provide even basic information about the three shootings during an immigration crackdown in Minneapolis, including two that were fatal.

title: Saudi Leader Is Said to Push Trump to Continue Iran War in Recent Calls
summary: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman sees a “historic opportunity” to remake the region, according to people briefed by U.S. officials on the conversations.

title: Trump’s Threat to ‘Obliterate’ Iran’s Power Stations Could Constitute a War Crime, Rights Experts Say
summary: Intentionally targeting the country’s energy infrastructure could constitute a war crime under international law.

title: College Graduates Are Facing the Grimmest Job Market in Years
summary: Artificial intelligence could reshape work, but for now a low-hire, low-fire labor market is the main impediment for young people seeking employment.

title: Covid Relief Loans Are Haunting Small Businesses
summary: The Small Business Administration lent $378 billion to keep businesses afloat. Getting paid back is proving difficult.

title: Wicked Stepmother No Longer, a Female Pharoah Gets a Reputational Makeover
summary: A reassessment of damaged 3,500-year-old statuary adds to evidence that Queen Hatshepsut wasn’t the villain that scholars long took her to be.

title: David A. Ross Sought Epstein’s Help to Build Art Museums. Now He’s Facing the Fallout.
summary: He mastered the world of the “Epstein Class” to build great museums. Now he’s confronting the cost.

title: Trump and Republican National Committee Lean Toward Dallas for Unusual 2026 GOP Midterm Convention
summary: Parties normally hold conventions every four years to nominate presidential candidates, but Republicans hope to hold one this year in the face of midterm headwinds.

title: Thomas Massie Thinks Being Hated by Trump Is ‘Worth It.’ Will Voters Agree?
summary: The Republican congressman from Kentucky is a die-hard libertarian who has centered his campaign on his willingness to buck the president. It has bought him the most expensive primary in the country.

title: California Governor’s Debate Canceled After Criticism Over Lack of Diversity
summary: The debate would have featured six candidates, all white. The inclusion of a low-polling mayor drew scrutiny in particular.

title: The U.S. Military Said It Helped Bomb a Drug Camp in Ecuador. It Was a Dairy Farm.
summary: The Times visited a village where the United States and Ecuador said they destroyed an armed group’s training camp. Residents said it was actually a dairy farm.

title: Russian Oil Shipment Puts Focus on Kremlin Spy Outpost in Cuba
summary: Moscow may be challenging President Trump’s effort to choke Cuba’s economy. China also has suspected listening posts on the island.

title: How Do You Measure Snow From Space? First, Climb a Mountain.
summary: A new satellite could transform how water is studied worldwide. But to help unlock its capabilities, scientists first needed to take critical measurements on a mountaintop.

title: Reality TV Confronts a Harsh TV Reality
summary: The number of unscripted series has plummeted by a third since 2022. As the industry rapidly changes, an era is quietly vanishing.

title: Comedy’s Most Manipulative Shot … and Its Greatest Weapon
summary: Audience reactions are a staple of standup specials. But they’re a strange device when you take a closer look.

title: Why Are So Many Democrats So Far Out of Touch?
summary: Candidates in safe districts are under no pressure to moderate in order to win.

title: The Deadly Gender Gap in Car Safety
summary: For over half a century, car safety standards have left women’s lives in the rearview.

title: Health Woes
summary: We look into the turmoil at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

title: Marco Rubio Testifies in Corruption Trial Against Former U.S. Rep. David Rivera
summary: To much ado, the secretary of state took the stand against David Rivera, a former congressman accused of secretly lobbying on behalf of the Venezuelan government.

title: NASA Adds Moon Base and Nuclear-Powered Mars Spacecraft to Road Map
summary: The agency’s leader said the plans and timelines for the coming decade aimed to make Americans “start believing again” in the mission of space exploration.

title: Afghanistan Frees American Dennis Coyle from Detention
summary: Dennis Walter Coyle, a researcher from Colorado, had been held since last year by the Taliban government.

title: Government Cuts Gut the Memory of Argentina’s Dirty War
summary: Fifty years after the military dictatorship, Argentina’s government is defunding human rights groups and promoting a revisionist account of the junta’s crimes.
